context: A tournament group match on neutral ground. The home-listed team entered as a clear rating favorite.
reveal_notes: A low-probability result is not evidence that the original forecast was wrong.

context: A World Cup semi-final hosted in the home-listed team's country between two elite teams.
reveal_notes: Independent Poisson models assign very little mass to extreme scorelines.

context: A final group match on neutral ground with qualification pressure.
reveal_notes: Game state and late goals can make the final score look less likely than the underlying match.

context: A continental final hosted by the home-listed team.
reveal_notes: A defensive tournament team can win without being the most probable winner in one match.

context: A World Cup final on neutral ground between two highly rated teams.
reveal_notes: The displayed result is after 90 minutes plus extra time for storytelling; the baseline remains a simplified match model.

context: A tournament group match on neutral ground between the previous finalists.
reveal_notes: A plausible away win and a five-goal away score are different forecasting questions.

context: A World Cup final on neutral ground between nearly equal elite teams.
reveal_notes: The challenge scores the regulation-plus-extra-time display result as a draw; production betting definitions differ.

context: A knockout match on neutral ground between a compact defensive team and a rating favorite.
reveal_notes: Tournament knockout matches can be lower scoring than a generic international baseline.

context: A knockout match on neutral ground with one team holding a substantial rating advantage.
reveal_notes: The model forecasts match outcomes, not penalty shootouts.

context: A continental final played at the home-listed team's national stadium.
reveal_notes: Finals can remain low scoring even when one side has a venue advantage.

context: A World Cup final on neutral ground with a moderate rating favorite.
reveal_notes: The baseline is transparent but does not model correlated score effects.

context: A knockout match on neutral ground with a clear rating favorite.
reveal_notes: A favorite can win while still experiencing a difficult match.

context: A World Cup final on neutral ground between two elite teams.
reveal_notes: Close elite matches naturally retain substantial draw probability.

context: A knockout match on neutral ground between similarly rated teams.
reveal_notes: Scoreline variance is much larger than winner-probability variance.

context: A World Cup final on neutral ground with the away-listed team holding a rating edge.
reveal_notes: A well-calibrated favorite still loses many individual matches.

context: A World Cup final played in the home-listed team's host country.
reveal_notes: The app explicitly separates model assumptions from historical narrative.

context: A continental final in the home-listed team's host country.
reveal_notes: Venue advantage changes probability, not certainty.

context: A continental final hosted by the home-listed team between close elite rivals.
reveal_notes: Finals often create tactical conditions not captured by a generic independent Poisson model.

context: A tournament group match on neutral ground with the home-listed team favored by ratings.
reveal_notes: Independent Poisson models commonly understate some low-score dependencies.

context: A World Cup semi-final on neutral ground between almost equally rated teams.
reveal_notes: Near-even ratings should produce broad, uncertain outcome probabilities.
